repo_signals: L3 smoke for platform.cloud.gcp: Cloud Run concurrency and min-instances, GKE autopilot, BigQuery slots and partitioning cost, service accounts and workload identity, IAM bindings, GCS, Cloud Functions

retrieval_query: gcp cloud run concurrency min instances gke bigquery slots partitioning workload identity iam binding service account gcs

validation_smoke.name: platform.cloud.gcp L3 validation smoke
signals: gcloud run services describe shows the tuned concurrency and minScale and the service reports no overload 429s
BigQuery dry-run reports bytes-scanned within the partition pruning budget for the target query
Workload Identity binding lets a GKE/Cloud Run workload call an API with no exported key present
IAM policy analyzer shows the principal holds only the documented predefined roles at the intended scope

diagnosis_smoke.name: platform.cloud.gcp diagnosis smoke
signals: Cloud Run cold starts traced to minScale 0 plus heavy container init rather than CPU throttling
BigQuery query slow or costly because it scans unpartitioned data or SELECTs * across a wide table
Permission error resolved to a missing role at the right resource node in the IAM hierarchy, not a broad grant
GKE pod stuck because Workload Identity is not bound or the node pool lacks the metadata server config
Cloud Run 429s traced to per-instance concurrency too low or maxScale clamping under burst
